soap:
RE-REVIEW · lance-format/lance#5583 — feat: implemented tokenizer plugins in rust
reviewed 45ac7c20 → 2ee3adeb  (branch DIVERGED: +620 / −8; base advanced, see note)

PACKET INTEGRITY NOTE (read first)
  The deterministic packet's scoped delta is unusable for this PR. `last_reviewed_sha` (45ac7c20) is NOT an ancestor of head — the branch diverged (ahead 620 / behind 8) and the base (main) jumped versions 2.0.0-beta.5 → 7.0.0-beta.2 (edition 2024, arrow 56→58, datafusion 50→53) in between. The scoper dropped 296 "off-branch" files, which silently swept out the actual tokenizer source (tokenizer.rs, plugin/{ffi,loader,tokenizer}.rs) — the very files my prior conditions live in. The packet also reconstructed 0 conditions. I reconstructed the ledger from my posted inline comments + the bot review, and verified each against the head blob of the real PR files (gh contents @ head), not the packet delta.

CONDITIONS LEDGER (4/7 met)
  ✓ met      [blocking] Return errors via an out-param, not get_error (thread-safe)
             → header LanceError* out-param on every fallible fn (lance_tokenizer_plugin.h L86–122); Rust threads &mut CError through all dispatch (loader.rs:1188,1211,1231; ffi.rs CError L1831)
  ✓ met      [blocking] Drop null-terminated strings; use a zero-copy string-ref struct
             → LanceStringRef in header (L37–40); CStringRef #[repr(C)] {data,length:u32} (ffi.rs:1776), with u32-overflow rejection (ffi.rs:1786–1806)
  ✓ met      [blocking] Cache the factory only (factory load is ~MB-expensive)
             → factory: Arc<OwnedPluginFactory> shared across Clones (tokenizer.rs:838,845,871–879); one factory per PluginTokenizer, reused by every FTS worker
  ✓ met      [blocking] Unify plugin into build_base_tokenizer
             → "plugin" => self.build_plugin_tokenizer() is now an arm of build_base_tokenizer (tokenizer.rs:499), not an early return; full filter pipeline in build() applies (L388–416)
  ✗ not_met  [blocking] Add comments to the proto plugin fields (resolution rules)   (author marked resolved + commented "I added some comments in proto" — NOT confirmed)
             → at head, protos/index_old.proto L42–43 are bare `optional string tokenizer_plugin_library = 12;` / `tokenizer_plugin_config = 13;` with ZERO doc comments. The claimed comments are absent from the head blob. (Doc moved into Rust docstrings instead, but the proto — the cross-library contract the condition targeted — is undocumented.)
  ✗ not_met  [blocking] (bot) Recoverable plugin errors must not panic the process
             → fixed on the fallible chain (loader.rs:1457–1476,1511–1538). BUT the tantivy TokenStream adapter still panics on recoverable plugin failures: PluginTokenStreamAdapter::new panics (tokenizer.rs:943,946); advance() panics on a negative next_token code (tokenizer.rs:999–1004); extract_token_text panics on NULL/!utf8 (tokenizer.rs:962,974). Documented as deliberate "fail-loud" because LanceTokenizer/TokenStream expose no Result. Still a process-level abort on a non-malicious plugin's runtime error — mitigated by design, not met.
  ~ moot     [blocking] (bot) Plugin path silently bypasses the filter pipeline + lance_tokenizer wrapping
             → superseded: plugin now runs through build_base_tokenizer, so build()'s filters AND the doc-type wrapping all apply (tokenizer.rs:416–425). plugin() intentionally turns default filters OFF (tokenizer.rs:376–386) but they're re-enableable; pinned by test_plugin_builder_{disables,allows_reenabling}_standard_filters. The "silent bypass" no longer exists.

FRESH-EYES DELTA
  issue (non-blocking): A plugin that returns a recoverable error MID-STREAM during a real FTS index build will panic the worker (tokenizer.rs advance() L999–1004; new() L943,946). The fallible-chain rework correctly returns Err up to PluginTokenizer::new, but once token streaming starts there is no Result channel, so a transient plugin fault aborts the in-flight build/search task. Documented and intentional; worth a follow-up. Not blocking.
  praise: The unsafe story is unusually disciplined for FFI — REQUIRED_VTABLE_CALLBACKS compile-time size assertion (loader.rs:1107–1114); api_version read before any other field (loader.rs:1063–1095); compile_fail doctests pinning the borrow chain (loader.rs:1270–1306); poison-recovery in Drop.
  No new correctness regressions found in code changed since last review.

RECOMMENDATION: comment (block on the proto-doc condition if treated strictly)
  The two genuine substance conditions and both ABI conditions are MET in the head blob. The proto doc-comment condition is NOT met and was marked resolved on a false claim — at minimum send it back. The panic-on-recoverable-error condition is mitigated on the fallible chain but remains by-design in the stream adapter; accept as documented or require a follow-up issue.
  confidence: high on met/not_met (verified against head blobs). what would make this wrong: if the proto doc comments live in a different proto file than index_old.proto, or if the attending considers the documented fail-loud adapter policy a full discharge of the panic finding.
